body:
| | | | |

Friend,

**Democrat-led states are struggling.** Right now, across the country, blue states are continuing to face stay at home policies, excessive regulation, and watching as businesses flee to red states in search of economic freedom.

After watching the constant failures in states like California and New York we want to know what you think. [**How would you grade the work Democrat governors are doing for their states?**](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001pgNfT8nedipPBYOWWqhVlnul5RwUWBq60YtlOuX4o3go3ZZd-BlBC1qEw_3kjJuCO4mK2RHGv5ngvYD5N_eKioNs-w8AhXnAdQ-agdFoH_QBgT2712E7wgXRe5eWtZM0HErjuQPlF_6_JbyDyUKiwOAhbKFwfU_Svay9lrQee4Ir4-c9itDq3u2JHjmtHQq99XB4BfObpT3sHso5KZzFbjl2ZEVoNs22vSJCZyJ9Spl_picFHSQiOTtT7JYPjjlb2xRDtaQdbrNpKAn-lEj4M-6MTu4lTpKLGsGx5oM2qQ4HP-5NL5ViG6awKTcCZ41baj8RjrcVc3LidpH7ZCY9y6gzkxK_bdlyNjfxR-BTHg0hIlJA86_i1oUolyNKi8zLcGZYTboUFhNWSFvBK3oKdDzD_YD2FR6FFpQ3nJwdTrt34aRRBMJT0A==&c=TKSQMNZM92FVEUQ1_0WgVqVHeeGCyLTgVUc5rn50GVAdSulgb6HVdg==&ch=iKGEvrqjXTsbxeIwLmBVtra8TT4l_pZVxyiQyDkyjTafS5aZBP703Q==)

There's a reason families and businesses are moving to red states. They know GOP governors will fight for them!

It's time every single American has a governor who will defend their rights and protect their livelihoods! **Democrat governors are not up to the job. They continue to shut down their states and walk away when things get tough.**

For months we've seen blue states struggle, it's time we tell Democrat governors how they're doing. **[Take two minutes to grade Democrat governors on their lack of leadership for their states.](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001pgNfT8nedipPBYOWWqhVlnul5RwUWBq60YtlOuX4o3go3ZZd-BlBC1qEw_3kjJuCO4mK2RHGv5ngvYD5N_eKioNs-w8AhXnAdQ-agdFoH_QBgT2712E7wgXRe5eWtZM0HErjuQPlF_6_JbyDyUKiwOAhbKFwfU_Svay9lrQee4Ir4-c9itDq3u2JHjmtHQq99XB4BfObpT3sHso5KZzFbjl2ZEVoNs22vSJCZyJ9Spl_picFHSQiOTtT7JYPjjlb2xRDtaQdbrNpKAn-lEj4M-6MTu4lTpKLGsGx5oM2qQ4HP-5NL5ViG6awKTcCZ41baj8RjrcVc3LidpH7ZCY9y6gzkxK_bdlyNjfxR-BTHg0hIlJA86_i1oUolyNKi8zLcGZYTboUFhNWSFvBK3oKdDzD_YD2FR6FFpQ3nJwdTrt34aRRBMJT0A==&c=TKSQMNZM92FVEUQ1_0WgVqVHeeGCyLTgVUc5rn50GVAdSulgb6HVdg==&ch=iKGEvrqjXTsbxeIwLmBVtra8TT4l_pZVxyiQyDkyjTafS5aZBP703Q==)**

**It 's time the people hold Democrat governors accountable for their constant failures.** Don't miss this opportunity to let them know what you think. Take our poll today to hold the dems accountable and show your support for GOP govs!

body:
We have stood together for the last 4 years. We cannot be divided now. If we do not unite now, the Democrats will do irreparable harm to our country. Can we count on you to join us in this fight?
Friend,
On his first day in office, President Joe Biden wasted no time at all in ERASING conservative advances from the last four years.

**Our best line of defense against the most radical left-wing agenda in America 's history is the Senate. Now more than ever, taking back control of the Senate Majority is a fight we can't lose! body:
The strong Conservative agenda that was passed to help Americans thrive is being rolled back before our eyes.

The course of our nation has been changed.

The strong Conservative agenda that was passed to help Americans thrive is being rolled back before our eyes.

**RESPONSE REQUESTED:**

We need to know what you think. Is Biden's agenda wrong for America?

[**Please submit your feedback by midnight tonight so we can compile a strategy and send it over to GOP leadership. Your input will help us restore our majority and fight for a strong Conservative agenda.**](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0010Zdr6er8zs7VFXnb5NtuZljZyhAFEO7b4vtGBzxeGBxZQYC9TQXw2cdeST8haWObtSzbyrrtFgXZwv9ba5uDE0DhK6pCUKFtie1NPa7oVlU0KS-S--a-gxbxXb7SNntSVBHnyb7rTRtoVLzfqKC5-bL_t00ViGiyLbbuxZBE0vzIfQw9NWAGV_7Kw0wgSz4k6no-R9M9Q6vtnxYC2A4RZutcOlrUks9SozBzCn2KmXijnlqpNTpaUpa-i2kMC_Yf-l2b5Gq80hoE_SkIDliQn6YzjKurVraBSvcHYkB607KF2zBFuQR2yrZapGjDoB_73HcQYBgPUm4MvkHjgKs0z0vgBRthuD41YGrSmyvGzMLT4pqoRkTipuQ4alv2_DA9FDecWRxM9a9JvyYrnxMPPIxQIfrdJzSrpuAiS8iLyYDzG37jCAUm4Zg9BhyIbr91LMAAn2As3Pc_KUCLTz8W4w==&c=HI4mTH8j3-h6Hk_RaDRjH6wPXMriFD5UWqyZayuDsbpkQRLR6m3C8w==&ch=vA2YccbLn0i0_J3Ud5Nefydv0CGZPyhSmcp-FhH4JXg9LX6SrK-5XA==)

******[Tell Us Now](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0010Zdr6er8zs7VFXnb5NtuZljZyhAFEO7b4vtGBzxeGBxZQYC9TQXw2cdeST8haWObtSzbyrrtFgXZwv9ba5uDE0DhK6pCUKFtie1NPa7oVlU0KS-S--a-gxbxXb7SNntSVBHnyb7rTRtoVLzfqKC5-bL_t00ViGiyLbbuxZBE0vzIfQw9NWAGV_7Kw0wgSz4k6no-R9M9Q6vtnxYC2A4RZutcOlrUks9SozBzCn2KmXijnlqpNTpaUpa-i2kMC_Yf-l2b5Gq80hoE_SkIDliQn6YzjKurVraBSvcHYkB607KF2zBFuQR2yrZapGjDoB_73HcQYBgPUm4MvkHjgKs0z0vgBRthuD41YGrSmyvGzMLT4pqoRkTipuQ4alv2_DA9FDecWRxM9a9JvyYrnxMPPIxQIfrdJzSrpuAiS8iLyYDzG37jCAUm4Zg9BhyIbr91LMAAn2As3Pc_KUCLTz8W4w==&c=HI4mTH8j3-h6Hk_RaDRjH6wPXMriFD5UWqyZayuDsbpkQRLR6m3C8w==&ch=vA2YccbLn0i0_J3Ud5Nefydv0CGZPyhSmcp-FhH4JXg9LX6SrK-5XA==)******

The destruction of Conservative values, the promise of burdensome regulations, and the reality of socialism seeping into the fabric of our nation are all now on the path of America's future.

During the first 100 days of Biden's administration, his liberal plans, laced in false hope, have the potential for higher taxes, bigger government, and economic failure.

[**Are Biden 's plans for his first 100 days helpful to America or a stumbling block for our nation's future? What is the Republican State Leadership Committee (RSLC)?
The Republican State Leadership Committee (RSLC) isn't just another alphabet soup political committee. We're the largest caucus of Republican state leaders in the nation with one unified goal: winning. Through innovative strategies and record-breaking support across all 50 states, we recruit, train, and support Republicans to state offices. Following a historically successful 2020 election cycle, state Republicans currently hold 62 of 99 state legislative chambers, 25 of 45 lieutenant governor seats, 28 of 50 secretary of state offices, and 11 of 12 independently-elected agriculture officials -- thanks in large part to the support from patriots like you. You can learn more about us at [rslc.gop](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0010MLzWtflr7FD_dAorTMDSJd0Vb9FsLFT5-0lBcMEOj2KKLbMtKmzDw6Glvv2rBO-i8e-8ntwI5o4mmb22WpJ1gaNCRqfIxF_e6IhUgBA2IJZ3x9ws32_rClJF9UFcRa1fPOdBBaiICM=&c=ht1Yq_Dpr7Mrmc1pPAEp26IcWyz5_rL1pCwKRu8yiH8zLIAt0NDTkw==&ch=glqSgs2Ir_kal1hBJHaWyOSkGdvyJB2yMRdv6T62M9P4AWuWiT1OSw==).

name: Team Rita
subject: Count all the votes
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rita Hart for Iowa
body:
| | | [](https://act.myngp.com/el/rzgtbxif8ZGRZ8cBUrJdI_6oXpW38hCXJ9CsPW_TbZA=/SAeRKWJ3kjs9lsCzlZKPlQB1QLw17833mgj_sDBjHEc=?refcode=210121-email) Peter -- We think this is pretty straightforward: In an election, you count all the votes. But yesterday, Rita's opponent asked the House of Representatives to block the election recount and disenfranchise at least 22 Iowa voters. **They are trying to stop the counting of legal votes because they are afraid that when all the votes are counted, Rita will have won this race.** [_**Chip in here to make sure we get an accurate recount in this incredibly close race. >>**_](https://act.myngp.com/el/rzgtbxif8ZGRZ8cBUrJdI_6oXpW38hCXJ9CsPW_TbZA=/SAeRKWJ3kjs9lsCzlZKPlQB1QLw17833mgj_sDBjHEc=?refcode=210121-email) Peter, this is the closest federal election in recent history and we _have_ to get it right. Almost two dozen Iowans are still waiting to have their votes counted and **thousands** have not had their ballots examined, so we're asking for your help today. [_**Can you chip in even $10 or $20 to help Rita make sure these votes are counted and we get a final tally in this election?**_](https://act.myngp.com/el/rzgtbxif8ZGRZ8cBUrJdI_6oXpW38hCXJ9CsPW_TbZA=/SAeRKWJ3kjs9lsCzlZKPlQB1QLw17833mgj_sDBjHEc=?refcode=210121-email) We can't let Republicans pick and choose who gets their votes counted. This fight is taking longer than we anticipated, but it's a fight we're going to win in the end with your help. All our best, -Team Rita | Paid for by Rita Hart for Iowa

| Friends, Last November our state overwhelmingly chose to put the Republicans in charge of the NH Senate. We promised to bring the principles of smaller government and protecting your freedoms to Concord. **That is why ****this week we are releasing our 2021 Action Plan - "Rebuilding a Stronger New Hampshire!"** [Read Our Full 2021 Action Plan Here!](https://click.mlsend2.com/link/c/YT0xNjA0MzI2NTY1MTU4NTg5ODM5JmM9dDZwNiZlPTE5MjgmYj01MDU5Njg4MDUmZD1wMHIydDZl.Hgitb2p0O4AVQd_lsX-BfXnVMJIW7iN_BWuL2fphfyU) We need to take the lessons we learned in 2020 and use them to rebuild our state. We have an opportunity to bring New Hampshire back from the pandemic in better shape than ever; with a stronger economy and more opportunities for all Granite Staters and all New Hampshire families. Please take a look at our plan. If you agree that we need to:

* help our working families,

clean_body: She will make LA-05 proud! &zwnj == == Friend, It is my honor to endorse Julia Letlow for Congress to represent Louisiana's 5th district. Julia is running to continue the mission started by her late husband Luke, who passed away due to coronavirus just days before he was supposed to be sworn into Congress. Julia has always served her community by advocating for higher education in Louisiana, but now she wants to take things one step further. She is more motivated than ever to deliver real results that help the people across her district and move Louisiana forward by putting an end to the chaos and dysfunction in Congress! == == Together, I know that Julia will join our team's effort to make sure we protect our farmers and agriculture industry, promote accessible higher education, allow small businesses to thrive, and ensure our communities have what they need to succeed. But we don 't have much time \-- Julia's special election is coming up in March! She needs all the support she can get in order to keep this seat red! == == == == == == == Thank you for your support, Friend, Congresswoman Ashley Hinson | PAID FOR BY ASHLEY HINSON FOR CONGRESS PO BOX 811, MARION, IA 52302 == | | ASHLEY HINSON FOR CONGRESS | PO BOX 811, Marion, IA 52302 | | Sent by mailto:action@standupforliberty.net powered by

name: Tim Scott
subject: Are we losing the American Dream?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TIM SCOTT FOR SENATE
body:
Friend,

_**Are we losing the American Dream?**_ This is the question that keeps me up at night.

As a conservative Black man from North Charleston, South Carolina whose family went from picking cotton to serving in Congress in one lifetime, I know that I have been blessed to live the American Dream.

But I'm worried future generations won't have the same opportunities to succeed.

If the Democrats get their way and pass a radical socialist agenda this year, our country will inevitably decline. That is why I'm doing everything in my power to stop them.

**[Friend, will you help me stop the Democrats and save the American Dream?](https://secure.winred.com/tim-scott-for-senate/save-the-american-dream/?utm_campaign=20210122_TY.100609_tim-scott_t1046397-1260&utm_medium=email&utm_source=TY.100609&utm_content=20210122_are-we-losing-the-american-dream/)

I need your help to remain a strong conservative voice in the Senate and stop the Democrats' socialist plans, which would ruin our country.**

I know Republicans like me are going to keep defending the American Dream. It is so important that we work together now, because we cannot give up any more ground.

[**Will you help me defend the American Dream by making a small donation today? **](https://secure.winred.com/tim-scott-for-senate/save-the-american-dream/?utm_campaign=20210122_TY.100609_tim-scott_t1046397-1260&utm_medium=email&utm_source=TY.100609&utm_content=20210122_are-we-losing-the-american-dream/)

The future of our country --_for generations to come_ \-- could depend on what we do right now.

Tim Scott

PAID FOR BY TIM SCOTT FOR SENATE

1405 ASHLEY RIVER ROAD
CHARLESTON, SC 29407

name: Tim Burchett
subject: The Next Four Years 🇺🇸
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Burchett for Congress**
body:
TIMtalk will keep you updated on the important issues and goings-on in our campaign, in Tennessee, and in our country. [](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=2635dd7164ee4ddf86ef362813af426e&_e=CjovfxhrigDJQhFG95Jx-T3a3mhmsdBoM-2BafEt5vSX9OwV-TcA9uz5k6ptRQWF2KqOLxDxyVb3J_gbQWvRy2vnD_k9fzpsaS2lw5Hwz0zzzyxPP2dSKJPxD9aplwspUJ1PTN8TFUgPiNIeF5msHti_zHAhBRN5uSFJszWMxyMqBn2bnsFdVwebAxvcxuDuAGRhMtlrPrNTM58x5_mbpcfh7dZU_dkdSmnr3fRjYhiD48PCyQJBDVjg-TMvF1FWhP89FX-ctY6GjbXXdiOBu2Wqj13g8pU-vrE7GWB-Zps%3D) **The Next Four Years 🇺🇸** _Photo courtesy of the New York Times_ On January 20, Joe Biden was inaugurated as the 46th president of the United States of America. **It is a testament to our democracy that every four years our country witnesses a peaceful transition of power.** I wish the incoming administration success and pray it continues to put America’s interests first. Our democratic system is something to celebrate, but it doesn’t mean we won’t continue to fight for conservative policies. Already, President Biden has announced plans for [amnesty legislation](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=2635dd7164ee4ddf86ef362813af426e&_e=CjovfxhrigDJQhFG95Jx-YoRc1B_7YQdYdTyuBw6Bav8tELLVzOD77IupHt6f1lW6v6jbrlCeUYDyeoFTi85P4-CUy3hUSIX8tdqf7qnCShOb3EbjL_1dWHWEau9OImlcMUJdZWPn3nirhYDltaWd-9xZwwJUi16d1BcG00mmlMFTA-aYUjC_P1WM7qhK36OMCCsXNb-JLSDLce3QUb5iaOmKrT9jwgTXjrTng3gb07euBBFtaQ4FLAPWhlWs2fA9UPCuhTsaERGqpydw024ecGtlbkK2LG7yy3s1qwDQWaRVJC5KofPFxq77CiA9_W4Ntv96aNi1W7Vm-ZeLTzVtb5-qw5t4Ks0L6KZgiJIjXdAtcBFpWEuHgk5RHGDqlf2RlHgT_t8pLzNaCT_bxqjmQ%3D%3D) and has [rejoined](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=2635dd7164ee4ddf86ef362813af426e&_e=CjovfxhrigDJQhFG95Jx-dI_yktzpIUh0s6qf_AHUFPzt9oZY_GyFS90QBPzc-VxJ3qge_lqVrnzgbTFrEzjM_oSE14RmdcLEO4_dtfyg_FWo2vbwSXsXm4g2yq4iNo2DXs53es2VY9Ddn2ebuNPKI5U_BHvmHTfcHuhzGBxh7jn0vcObzdG5aeZEigdutGhTZkb91xEPwZkE1XcctExAGUkPvlZHHRs-KzsCkCnnouh-sDWKKwWm3CfMNpON0GeC1QTKyjiNoZrAznEy9K22L-g8B9aPo28bFgaspDjWY_HC6wG3xmbJ9jvDkeEjUYdKpwhA-gFD9CQ-1w9KfZ2mfIjKL5oJ2JThRCs3xzwN4Q%3D) the job-killing Paris Climate Agreement. It’s up to us to shine a light on bad ideas and hold the administration accountable. We can also work together on issues we agree on. I was encouraged to hear Biden’s nominee for Secretary of State [agree with the Trump administration’s declaration](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=2635dd7164ee4ddf86ef362813af426e&_e=CjovfxhrigDJQhFG95Jx-f82DU9vyCEybmLHCLT4LFet3zxsm7bUAne_UWhp2thPPMvA6GKHFY86Bm78FKO-IQS8l7ejUYbClM9VEnj502z4ZURh5BxbiRAnv4sUbcKO37PlkP7oPsKVsdTr0CoBAhFjG1Drf2HpQ1C_QmxrO-JoGh8jrgoCklJaPfIvSopEA-8eNtHZZiOxQm13CpKEs1qYaJ2VmD3t35P2wiEF9pEQpV-bA3A7RksI2asvJprhFXpFPQ0zET1ezoUiRLM6AUcVaESM7TkVUpph7XVGInoD2P1WLIjjzEFM_xoRyzMr) that China is guilty of “genocide and crimes against humanity.” China is a major national security threat, and it’s about time we call a spade a spade. | |
---
**Paying Tribute to a Real Hero 🎖️**
**Last week, we lost a real hero.** Sgt. Charlie Jackson Hamilton was a Korean War veteran who passed away at age 90. Our office helped him get his war medals, which he rightly earned. It’s an honor to represent a region that is home to so many people who fought for our country. Please say a prayer for Charlie and his family today.
|
---
**Bicycle Ministry 🚴**
_Photo courtesy of the Robbie Pruitt_

Talk about making lemonade from lemons: Pastor Robbie Pruitt was [disappointed when his bike was stolen](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=feefb5ad83f24ba4b99ac997c1b47e86&_m=2635dd7164ee4ddf86ef362813af426e&_e=CjovfxhrigDJQhFG95Jx-bhgeevlvK323rCtkDL0MKWcjDjTY2gvFcJWA9VJMe3E1EcQHhoh9T9-YNz6Vnu8i9TjWzUOZATaauhL7IBJgLcSPhvcbGZ8WsYjidjL74zrWveqMK0XyVeF5S-TSj3qesmpWgKzEDXvf6oPuyrQe-16u9ZSM0ps-xKJLkzdqReZBs2bYWXEPjviXZAfkX00SwN35aPul0JznFF-GxO4UGAIrL2d2i46Hipwl3pHkD-kMuFNKwpOrhNk6o1LHKbhXA0jGpo9JuVjQwN-QUOAcZ1HoKu-8HWwE3eSBP_cBuHVeYKeOxH3aV7NaBcFty3Wg5ehwhCbZ2zX4gsNcX7NBC0%3D). But he decided to make the most of it, taking it as a sign that bikes were in short supply. The assistant rector for a Virginia church invited families to donate old bikes and has now repaired more than 140. He gives them to needy families and calls the effort a "bicycle ministry."
|

name: =?utf-8?Q?Indiana=20Republican=20Party?=
subject: The Resiliency of Hoosiers
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Indiana Republican Party
body:
A historic week is coming to a close, and it is one that I won’t soon forget. I had the high honor to be among the supporters who welcomed Vice Pre... | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/indiana/the-resiliency-of-hoosiers?e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| |
---
|
---
|
---
| _**The Resiliency of Hoosiers**_
A historic week is coming to a close, and it is one that I won’t soon forget. I had the high honor to be among the supporters who welcomed **Vice President and Second Lady Mike and Karen Pence** back home again to Indiana. On behalf of Hoosiers throughout our state, I thanked Mike and Karen for their exemplary service to our state and nation these last four years. As Vice President, Mike Pence served with the character, courage, and honor that we’ve all known him for and that was instilled in him growing up in Columbus.

We also heard Governor Holcomb deliver his annual State of the State address. Delivered virtually this year, the governor laid out a bold agenda for Indiana’s future and reminded Hoosiers of our firm foundation. This foundation, laid by Governor Holcomb, Pence, Daniels, and other Republican leaders these last several years, has Indiana in a position to bounce back from the pandemic quickly and continue growing for all Hoosiers. Governor Holcomb is setting up 2021 to be a year of recovery, growth, and continued optimism because Hoosiers are resilient and our future is bright.

The New Year is in full swing, the legislative session is heating up and your Indiana Republican Party will continue our relentless pursuit of positive conservative results for our state. To borrow a line from The Who, [“I’ve got a feeling ’21 is going to be a good year.”](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=9e66a48773&e=1acd9bb3d6)

Thanks
Kyle
---
|
---
| | **Governor Holcomb's Week That Was**
---
|
---
| _**Holcomb Delivers State of the State**_ It was a busy week for Governor Holcomb, one that started with the governor paying tribute to Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. The governor encouraged all Hoosiers to honor Dr. King’s legacy by continuing to work together to bend the arc of history further toward equity. We’ve linked to the governor’s video message below.

The governor also delivered his fifth [State of the State](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=0d3bfd1d47&e=1acd9bb3d6) Address. After winning re-election with the most votes for a gubernatorial candidate in Indiana history, this was the first State of the State of the second term. It was also the first State of the State to be delivered from a television studio instead of from the Indiana House chamber, a reminder of how much COVID-19 still continues to impact our lives.

To stay up to date on the latest developments, make sure you're following** Governor Holcomb** on [Facebook](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=43b6234948&e=1acd9bb3d6), [Twitter](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=3c870465f1&e=1acd9bb3d6), and [Instagram](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=37777556f7&e=1acd9bb3d6)!
---
|
---
|
---
| _**Thank you Vice President Pence**_ As Vice President Mike Pence and Second Lady Karen Pence departed from Washington on Wednesday, Hoosiers gathered in their home town of Columbus to thank them for their service. Vice President Pence served our country with honor and courage and as Hoosiers, we are forever thankful.
---
|
---
| _**State of the State Recap**_ On Tuesday, **Governor Holcomb** kicked off his second term by delivering his fifth [State of the State](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c4e2a95e1d&e=1acd9bb3d6) Address. He laid out a clear vision for Indiana's future that was highlighted by:
* His budget proposal. A ninth straight balanced budget that will allow Indiana to fully fund areas that are vital to our state’s continued success.
* Our state's COVID-19 response, which includes being the best in the Midwest for vaccine distribution.
* The Healthy Indiana Plan receiving a federal waver to continue covering eligible Hoosiers.
* Indiana's lowest recorded infant mortality rate in state history.
* Indiana being recognized as the number one state in the nation for increased adoptions from foster care.
* Protecting 100% of K-12 funding during the current school year.
* Being able to attract and retain teacher talent by becoming one of the best in the Midwest for teacher pay.
* Continuing to help all Hoosiers obtain post-secondary educations.
* Continuing to improve our state's world-class infrastructure to better connect Hoosiers in every town, big or small.
* Completing I-69 three years ahead of schedule.
* Steaming ahead with the West Lake Corridor Expansion & double tracking the South Shore Line. Two game-changing infrastructure projects in Northwest Indiana.
* Re-starting our state's investment in trails.
* Continuing to improve broadband connectivity for Hoosiers.
* Despite the pandemic, another record year of new job commitments and investment in Indiana.
* Continuing to provide opportunities to our state's veterans.
* Asking legislature to make Indiana the 31st state to pass a pregnancy accommodations bill.
* Continuing to modernize state government and make it more effective for all Hoosiers.

Under **Governor Holcomb's** leadership, Indiana has broken records and has become a true destination for people to live, work, play, and stay. To watch **Governor Holcomb's** the full [State of the State](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=712807e1c0&e=1acd9bb3d6) Address, click [here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f625737036&e=1acd9bb3d6).
_**"For Indiana, the future is now, and the world continues to hear Indiana’s engines roar. That’s why I’m more excited than ever to work together to build a state of opportunity for all Hoosiers." -Governor Holcomb**_ [\--> Click here for a full recap of Governor Holcomb's State of the State Address <\--](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f8453d6a52&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
|
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=72babbc5ca&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| _**Governor Holcomb's MLK Day Message**_ On Monday, **Governor Holcomb **honored the life of Dr. King by delivering a message of inclusion and equality for all Hoosiers. There's more work to do, but Governor Holcomb will continue working to create One Indiana for All. Watch the full message [here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f936dd771a&e=1acd9bb3d6).
_**"Reverend King accomplished more in his 39 years than just about anyone in the history of our country.. Together, we’ll honor his legacy by continuing his work and by bending the arc further toward equity." -Governor Holcomb**_

clean_body: A historic week is coming to a close, and it is one that I won’t soon forget. I had the high honor to be among the supporters who welcomed Vice Pre... | | | | | | | | The Resiliency of Hoosiers A historic week is coming to a close, and it is one that I won’t soon forget. I had the high honor to be among the supporters who welcomed Vice President and Second Lady Mike and Karen Pence back home again to Indiana. On behalf of Hoosiers throughout our state, I thanked Mike and Karen for their exemplary service to our state and nation these last four years. As Vice President, Mike Pence served with the character, courage, and honor that we’ve all known him for and that was instilled in him growing up in Columbus. We also heard Governor Holcomb deliver his annual State of the State address. Delivered virtually this year, the governor laid out a bold agenda for Indiana’s future and reminded Hoosiers of our firm foundation. This foundation, laid by Governor Holcomb, Pence, Daniels, and other Republican leaders these last several years, has Indiana in a position to bounce back from the pandemic quickly and continue growing for all Hoosiers. Governor Holcomb is setting up 2021 to be a year of recovery, growth, and continued optimism because Hoosiers are resilient and our future is bright. The New Year is in full swing, the legislative session is heating up and your Indiana Republican Party will continue our relentless pursuit of positive conservative results for our state. To borrow a line from The Who, Thanks Kyle | | | Governor Holcomb's Week That Was | | Holcomb Delivers State of the State It was a busy week for Governor Holcomb, one that started with the governor paying tribute to Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. The governor encouraged all Hoosiers to honor Dr. King’s legacy by continuing to work together to bend the arc of history further toward equity. We’ve linked to the governor’s video message below. The governor also delivered his fifth Address. After winning re-election with the most votes for a gubernatorial candidate in Indiana history, this was the first State of the State of the second term. It was also the first State of the State to be delivered from a television studio instead of from the Indiana House chamber, a reminder of how much COVID-19 still continues to impact our lives. To stay up to date on the latest developments, make sure you're following Governor Holcomb on , , and ! | | | Thank you Vice President Pence As Vice President Mike Pence and Second Lady Karen Pence departed from Washington on Wednesday, Hoosiers gathered in their home town of Columbus to thank them for their service. Vice President Pence served our country with honor and courage and as Hoosiers, we are forever thankful. | | State of the State Recap On Tuesday, Governor Holcomb kicked off his second term by delivering his fifth Address. He laid out a clear vision for Indiana's future that was highlighted by: His budget proposal. A ninth straight balanced budget that will allow Indiana to fully fund areas that are vital to our state’s continued success. Our state's COVID-19 response, which includes being the best in the Midwest for vaccine distribution. The Healthy Indiana Plan receiving a federal waver to continue covering eligible Hoosiers. Indiana's lowest recorded infant mortality rate in state history. Indiana being recognized as the number one state in the nation for increased adoptions from foster care. Protecting 100% of K-12 funding during the current school year. Being able to attract and retain teacher talent by becoming one of the best in the Midwest for teacher pay. Continuing to help all Hoosiers obtain post-secondary educations. Continuing to improve our state's world-class infrastructure to better connect Hoosiers in every town, big or small. Completing I-69 three years ahead of schedule. Steaming ahead with the West Lake Corridor Expansion & double tracking the South Shore Line. Two game-changing infrastructure projects in Northwest Indiana. Re-starting our state's investment in trails. Continuing to improve broadband connectivity for Hoosiers. Despite the pandemic, another record year of new job commitments and investment in Indiana. Continuing to provide opportunities to our state's veterans. Asking legislature to make Indiana the 31st state to pass a pregnancy accommodations bill. Continuing to modernize state government and make it more effective for all Hoosiers. Under Governor Holcomb's leadership, Indiana has broken records and has become a true destination for people to live, work, play, and stay. To watch Governor Holcomb's the full Address, click . "For Indiana, the future is now, and the world continues to hear Indiana’s engines roar. That’s why I’m more excited than ever to work together to build a state of opportunity for all Hoosiers." -Governor Holcomb | | | Governor Holcomb's MLK Day Message On Monday, Governor Holcomb honored the life of Dr. King by delivering a message of inclusion and equality for all Hoosiers. There's more work to do, but Governor Holcomb will continue working to create One Indiana for All. Watch the full message . "Reverend King accomplished more in his 39 years than just about anyone in the history of our country.. Together, we’ll honor his legacy by continuing his work and by bending the arc further toward equity." -Governor Holcomb | | | | Indiana is Second Best in the Nation in Vaccinations per 100 Residents Indiana is not only the best in the Midwest in vaccine distribution, but we're also second-best in the nation! Thanks to Governor Holcomb and Operation Warp Speed Indiana continues to set the bar and lead the way with COVID-19 vaccines. Not only is Indiana getting vaccines distributed quickly, but now Hoosiers 70+ can schedule to get vaccinated. | | News You Can Use Governor Eric Holcomb Lt. | | Friends-

So after two years of making promises to the American people, what does Joe Biden do on Day One?

His very first action was to help Communist China, at the expense of American workers and taxpayers.

I’m all for energy efficiency and reducing greenhouse gas emissions, but Biden is forcing American companies to incur massive new costs, while their competitors in China and India can do whatever they want—for the next 10 years.

THAT’S NOT FAIR! And it will cost us thousands of good paying jobs, with many of them belonging to local union members.

Biden had a responsibility to hold out and negotiate a better deal for America.

[I will never sell out our country and, with your help, I’ll keep fighting for our America First Agenda. (Don’t even get me started about what Biden’s doing on immigration!)](https://nicolemalliotakis.lt.acemlnc.com/Prod/link-tracker?redirectUrl=aHR0cHMlM0ElMkYlMkZzZWN1cmUud2lucmVkLmNvbSUyRm5pY29sZW1hbGxpb3Rha2lzJTJGZW1haWwlM0Z1dG1fc291cmNlJTNEZW1haWwlMjZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtJTNEYXBwZWFsJTI2dXRtX2NhbXBhaWduJTNESGlzJTJCRmlyc3QlMkJBY3QlMkJFbWFpbA==&a=25864240&account=nicolemalliotakis%2Eactivehosted%2Ecom&email=8RRDsQMegL%2Bk1g7KlTCN33wFoqDlMHNmyq65fGLdufk%3D&s=e671297fc19753f4b1f2ae7718aefd90&i=416A603A1A8270)

subject: BREAKING: Motion to dismiss filed
body:
| | | |
---
| Friends,

Yesterday, we filed a motion to dismiss Rita Hart's partisan move to steal this election from voters in Iowa's 2nd district.
---
| [Donate Now](https://millermeeks2020.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7d3bd416b094d33aae4029ad1&id=9d89e640fd&e=16fac31a0f "Donate Now")
---
| | | Long-standing precedent of the House of Representatives requires losing candidates to go through the state courts first before filing an election contest in the House. Rita Hart should have raised her claims before a neutral panel of Iowa judges rather than before a political process controlled by her own party.

Rita Hart was clearly afraid of bringing her case to state court. Her actions show that she knew her weak case would never have persuaded judges. She chose politics over law. She was wrong to do so and did a disservice to every Iowan who voted in this election. The House of Representatives should follow their own precedents and dismiss her contest.

We will not let Rita Hart overturn the will of Iowa voters, and we are definitely not going to let Nancy Pelosi undermine the integrity of Iowa elections. [Will you help us fight for Iowa by chipping in $25, $250 or even $100 today?](https://millermeeks2020.us20.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7d3bd416b094d33aae4029ad1&id=74d265c40c&e=16fac31a0f)

Thank you for your support!

Miller-Meeks Legal Team
---

| Each week, I will be compiling a collection of stories and articles from the previous week that you might find interesting. If you see what you like, please, share with your family and friend!

As Leader of the Senate Republican Conference, I made you all a promise. Every day of session, we would introduce legislation to curb the governor's emergency powers. So far, we have offered 4 different amendments, and Democrats have turned them down each and every time.
| |
---
| Last week, the Governor gave us his State of the State message, which seemed to be a rehash of ideas he got from the Senate Republican Conference and our “Reset NY” plan. Missed the launch of Reset NY? Check out the link below.

# _[**_Ortt & Republican conference plan to 'Reset New York'_**](https://RobOrtt.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8c02d052541198272b8fde278&id=88fae96e70&e=9f843ba602)_

|

| | __**In Case You Missed It**__
---
| [Editorial — Rival Voices: GOP Senators in Albany Call for Changes in State’s Policies](https://RobOrtt.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8c02d052541198272b8fde278&id=2b9f6139f3&e=9f843ba602 "Editorial — Rival Voices: GOP Senators in Albany Call for Changes in State’s Policies")

---
| [Cuomo Warns of Tax Hikes, Dire Cuts if Feds Can't Find $15B](https://RobOrtt.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8c02d052541198272b8fde278&id=d62bde84d0&e=9f843ba602 "Cuomo Warns of Tax Hikes, Dire Cuts if Feds Can't Find $15B")
---

---
|
---
| **_My Statement on Gov. Cuomo's 2021 Executive Budget Proposal_**
"On Tuesday, Gov. Cuomo presented his 2021 State Budget. Talk about passing the buck. There were absolutely no specifics, and it is all dependent on the federal government bailing out the bad business New York State has continued under the Cuomo administration. The Governor continues to shift blame to the federal government while Albany Democrats skirt responsibility and allow the Governor to make all of the decisions unilaterally. New York’s overtaxing and overspending far predate COVID, and it’s time for the state government to realize that on behalf of our struggling businesses and middle-class families."
---

name: Marco Rubio
subject: Genocide in China
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Marco Rubio for Senate
body:
Friend,

The Trump Administration did the right thing by calling the horrific crimes being committed by the Chinese Communist Party against Uyghurs and other Muslim minorities by their rightful name: **crimes against humanity and genocide.**

The ongoing crimes at the hands of the Chinese government is a human tragedy that demands an immediate international response. These determinations are a call to action for the incoming administration, Congress, and our allies. We must ensure that the U.S. and free nations do all we can to end these atrocities and ensure that it remains a priority of our nation's China policy.

name: Senator Chris Van Hollen
subject: Senate Update: Turning the Page
body:
| | | [Click here to open this e-mail in its own browser window](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?link=SenVanHollen%2501148950%2501January%2bFull%2bNewsletter%2b01-22-21%250115613.23734358.5038779.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501) | [Click here to open a plain text version of this email](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?plain_text=Y&link=SenVanHollen%2501148950%2501January%2bFull%2bNewsletter%2b01-22-21%250115613.23734358.5038779.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501)
---|---
| | | **January 22, 2021** |
---|---|---|---
| | | Earlier this week, we took time to [reflect](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15613.23734358.5038779.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2ftwitter.com%2fChrisVanHollen%2fstatus%2f1351216596384157696&redir_log=625813311242374) on the vision, the mission, and the dream of the Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. His birthday calls on us to reevaluate our progress in fulfilling what he called the "promissory note" of equal rights, equal justice, and equal opportunity in our nation. Dr. King often preached that "the arc of the moral universe is long, but it bends towards justice" - he also knew that it doesn't bend on its own. Each of us has a responsibility to participate in our democracy, confront social and racial injustice in all its forms, and bend that arc further and faster. As we turn the page on this dark chapter in American history, our country must move forward armed with the spirit and wisdom of Dr. King's message.
---
### Celebrating a New Day
Wednesday marked the beginning of a new chapter for our country. We witnessed the historic swearing-in of Joe Biden and Kamala Harris as our next President and Vice President of the United States. I was moved to watch Vice President Harris take her oath of office on the bible owned by a son of Baltimore and barrier-breaking Supreme Court Justice, Thurgood Marshall. And I was inspired by President Biden's call for unity over division, as he addressed our nation for the first time as Commander-In-Chief. President Biden and Vice President Harris have pledged to serve each and every American - both those who voted for them and those who didn't. While our nation now faces some of the most difficult challenges in our history, I am committed to working with the new Administration and my colleagues in Congress to meet these problems head-on. Together, we must work to heal our country, stride boldly into the future, and pursue a more perfect, just, and equal union.
### Tackling the Challenges at Hand
Our top priority must be to beat this pandemic and bring relief to those hardest hit by the economic fallout of this crisis. Marylanders are still struggling to make ends meet and are seeking action amid the growing challenges in vaccine rollout and the climbing death toll, which has now surpassed 400,000 in the United States. I'm glad that lawmakers came together in December to pass another bipartisan stimulus bill and was pleased to join the Maryland delegation last week to [announce](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15613.23734358.5038779.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fmaryland-delegation-members-announce-more-than-400-million-to-expand-statewide-covid-19-vaccine-distribution-contact-tracing-and-testing-capacity&redir_log=674636610771383) that $400 million will go to expanding our state's vaccine distribution, contact tracing, and testing capacity. But much work remains to be done, and we must pass more emergency relief now. Fighting the COVID-19 pandemic requires an all-hands-on-deck approach. I'm glad to see the Biden Administration hit the ground running with their national strategy to fully mobilize the federal government and improve coordination with state and local governments to overcome this pandemic. And I [support](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15613.23734358.5038779.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-statement-on-biden-national-strategy-to-tackle-the-pandemic-new-executive-actions&redir_log=976760988171021) President Biden's comprehensive plan, which will help us tackle the coronavirus head-on by boosting our capacity to vaccinate, test for, and treat this disease. From the start, I have urged the Executive Branch to make full use of the Defense Production Act to generate the necessary supplies to fight the pandemic, and I am glad President Biden is moving quickly to do so. Biden's strategy also includes support for our state and local governments - a priority I've repeatedly pressed for - by providing new funds and increasing the federal cost share for FEMA and National Guard assistance to 100 percent. Critically, this plan confronts the child care crisis, prioritizes the safe reopening of schools, and will provide our students and educators with more resources to improve learning at this challenging time. President Biden's plan also commits to addressing the disparate impact of this pandemic on communities of color and will help tackle these challenges by improving community-based services. Last but not least, this new strategy lays the groundwork to reopen our economy and build back better. I look forward to working with my colleagues in Congress to implement and build on this plan. I also [support](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15613.23734358.5038779.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-statement-on-new-biden-covid-19-plan&redir_log=912205413186691) President Biden's earlier-released American Rescue Plan, which will increase the $600 payments by an additional $1,400, bringing the total relief payment to $2,000 per eligible person, among other key provisions. It's time to move full-steam ahead. The coronavirus has also shone a harsh light on the deeper, structural challenges facing our nation that we must confront at their source. We need to build back better - with an emphasis on "better." I will fight to build a dynamic economy that works for everyone, root out systemic racism in all its forms, and address the threat of the climate crisis by investing in clean energy and making polluters pay - and I look forward to partnering with the Biden-Harris Administration to meet these pressing challenges with urgency. I'm pleased that the new Administration has already moved forward with executive actions to tackle the pandemic, promote equity, and protect our planet. Congress has a responsibility to pass legislation alongside these vital efforts, and I will work every day in the halls of the Senate to achieve that goal.
### Defying Insurrection
In order to march toward the future, we need to resolve the unfinished business of the past. Make no mistake: while serving as Commander-in-Chief, Donald Trump threatened the safety and security of our democracy, demonstrated contempt for our Constitution, and violated his oath to protect the American people and defend our republic. The Article of Impeachment passed in a bipartisan vote by the House of Representatives last week makes the case plain: Trump "engaged in high Crimes and Misdemeanors by inciting violence against the Government of the United States." His conduct left the Congress with no choice but to take action, and I [stand](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15613.23734358.5038779.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-statement-on-house-impeachment&redir_log=703457317958666) with the House in its decision to impeach. Now, his case goes to the United States Senate, and I believe lawmakers of both parties have a moral duty to hold Trump accountable. I believe accountability and unity go hand-in-hand. As President Biden said in his inaugural address, "We must reject a culture in which facts themselves are manipulated and even manufactured... Each of us has a duty and responsibility, as citizens, as Americans, and especially as leaders, leaders who have pledged to honor our Constitution and protect our nation, to defend the truth and defeat the lies." Joining forces and working across the aisle to achieve that goal will help our nation come together again. I am convinced that our brightest days are still to come. Chris Van Hollen
[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15613.23734358.5038779.7517223&report_id=Facebook&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.facebook.com%2fchrisvanhollen%2f&redir_log=328155479639284 "Facebook")[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15613.23734358.5038779.7517223&report_id=Twitter&redirect=https%3a%2f%2ftwitter.com%2fChrisVanHollen&redir_log=957640916473241 "Twitter")[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15613.23734358.5038779.7517223&report_id=Instagram&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.instagram.com%2fchrisvanhollen%2f&redir_log=389295287187292 "Instagram")[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15613.23734358.5038779.7517223&report_id=YouTube&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fuser%2fRepChrisVanHollen&redir_log=463627374976326 "YouTube")[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15613.23734358.5038779.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fmedium.com%2f%40ChrisVanHollen&redir_log=575666539451239)
|

clean_body: | | | | ---|--- | | | January 22, 2021 | ---|---|---|--- | | | Earlier this week, we took time to on the vision, the mission, and the dream of the Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. His birthday calls on us to reevaluate our progress in fulfilling what he called the "promissory note" of equal rights, equal justice, and equal opportunity in our nation. Dr. King often preached that "the arc of the moral universe is long, but it bends towards justice" - he also knew that it doesn't bend on its own. Each of us has a responsibility to participate in our democracy, confront social and racial injustice in all its forms, and bend that arc further and faster. As we turn the page on this dark chapter in American history, our country must move forward armed with the spirit and wisdom of Dr. King's message. Celebrating a New Day Wednesday marked the beginning of a new chapter for our country. We witnessed the historic swearing-in of Joe Biden and Kamala Harris as our next President and Vice President of the United States. I was moved to watch Vice President Harris take her oath of office on the bible owned by a son of Baltimore and barrier-breaking Supreme Court Justice, Thurgood Marshall. And I was inspired by President Biden's call for unity over division, as he addressed our nation for the first time as Commander-In-Chief. President Biden and Vice President Harris have pledged to serve each and every American - both those who voted for them and those who didn't. While our nation now faces some of the most difficult challenges in our history, I am committed to working with the new Administration and my colleagues in Congress to meet these problems head-on. Together, we must work to heal our country, stride boldly into the future, and pursue a more perfect, just, and equal union. Tackling the Challenges at Hand Our top priority must be to beat this pandemic and bring relief to those hardest hit by the economic fallout of this crisis. Marylanders are still struggling to make ends meet and are seeking action amid the growing challenges in vaccine rollout and the climbing death toll, which has now surpassed 400,000 in the United States. I'm glad that lawmakers came together in December to pass another bipartisan stimulus bill and was pleased to join the Maryland delegation last week to that $400 million will go to expanding our state's vaccine distribution, contact tracing, and testing capacity. But much work remains to be done, and we must pass more emergency relief now. Fighting the COVID-19 pandemic requires an all-hands-on-deck approach. I'm glad to see the Biden Administration hit the ground running with their national strategy to fully mobilize the federal government and improve coordination with state and local governments to overcome this pandemic. And I President Biden's comprehensive plan, which will help us tackle the coronavirus head-on by boosting our capacity to vaccinate, test for, and treat this disease. From the start, I have urged the Executive Branch to make full use of the Defense Production Act to generate the necessary supplies to fight the pandemic, and I am glad President Biden is moving quickly to do so. Biden's strategy also includes support for our state and local governments - a priority I've repeatedly pressed for - by providing new funds and increasing the federal cost share for FEMA and National Guard assistance to 100 percent. Critically, this plan confronts the child care crisis, prioritizes the safe reopening of schools, and will provide our students and educators with more resources to improve learning at this challenging time. President Biden's plan also commits to addressing the disparate impact of this pandemic on communities of color and will help tackle these challenges by improving community-based services. Last but not least, this new strategy lays the groundwork to reopen our economy and build back better. I look forward to working with my colleagues in Congress to implement and build on this plan. I also President Biden's earlier-released American Rescue Plan, which will increase the $600 payments by an additional $1,400, bringing the total relief payment to $2,000 per eligible person, among other key provisions. It's time to move full-steam ahead. The coronavirus has also shone a harsh light on the deeper, structural challenges facing our nation that we must confront at their source. We need to build back better - with an emphasis on "better." I will fight to build a dynamic economy that works for everyone, root out systemic racism in all its forms, and address the threat of the climate crisis by investing in clean energy and making polluters pay - and I look forward to partnering with the Biden-Harris Administration to meet these pressing challenges with urgency. I'm pleased that the new Administration has already moved forward with executive actions to tackle the pandemic, promote equity, and protect our planet. Congress has a responsibility to pass legislation alongside these vital efforts, and I will work every day in the halls of the Senate to achieve that goal. Defying Insurrection In order to march toward the future, we need to resolve the unfinished business of the past. Make no mistake: while serving as Commander-in-Chief, Donald Trump threatened the safety and security of our democracy, demonstrated contempt for our Constitution, and violated his oath to protect the American people and defend our republic. The Article of Impeachment passed in a bipartisan vote by the House of Representatives last week makes the case plain: Trump "engaged in high Crimes and Misdemeanors by inciting violence against the Government of the United States." His conduct left the Congress with no choice but to take action, and I with the House in its decision to impeach. Now, his case goes to the United States Senate, and I believe lawmakers of both parties have a moral duty to hold Trump accountable. I believe accountability and unity go hand-in-hand. As President Biden said in his inaugural address, "We must reject a culture in which facts themselves are manipulated and even manufactured... Each of us has a duty and responsibility, as citizens, as Americans, and especially as leaders, leaders who have pledged to honor our Constitution and protect our nation, to defend the truth and defeat the lies." Joining forces and working across the aisle to achieve that goal will help our nation come together again. I am convinced that our brightest days are still to come. name: Dr. Philip Chan, RIDOH
subject: Vaccine Update
body:
---
| | | | | | Hello friend,
I'm writing today to share an update on where we stand in our COVID-19 vaccination campaign. If you find this information helpful, [please sign up for the Rhode Island Department of Health RIDOH's weekly update that is dedicated to the COVID-19 vaccine](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-ft4xkKCSBx8WvrTjurTymyvPkppZxmyvxTl9PG9FhoTIHSsncm_qnGKv7eNuRYw-9HFLKNXoO3LEPbDaLdAMpaxSDlWA36LTBQQO72dQ-tOH9ipVUNqpDb5XJ90n5KmCuYFf3H0MXWNEU189H-oi_64Ro_OgfAj&c=A1_MvXMmrIevMJQwWTCm-CDieowSt_XU8xkuhC7_oI72FLQ-kYhP5A==&ch=rEklWNiYKABmwLJfXmF8FQZRNmtJjqMvtmyFHH2CL93ZoqtWkoAx5w==).
As of this morning, 66,070 doses of vaccine had been administered in Rhode Island (52,925 first doses, and 13,145 second doses). We are working hard to distribute vaccine, but supply remains very limited. Right now, we're receiving enough first doses each week for about 1.5% of our population. While other states are in the same position, Rhode Island ranks among the top states nationally in terms of the rate of second doses administered.
We are currently in Phase 1 of our vaccination campaign. The aim of Phase 1 has been to ensure the stability of our healthcare system and to protect people in congregate living settings, as well as to start vaccinating in some of our hardest hit areas.
When it comes to older adults, we began vaccinating in nursing homes in December. This week, we started to vaccinate in assisted living facilities and other congregate living settings. By the middle of February, we expect the vaccine will be available for adults 75 and older. We have received a lot of questions about vaccination of older adults. In Rhode Island, there are 187,000 Rhode Islanders age 65 or older. Since we are only getting about 14,000 first doses of vaccine a week, we are taking a stepwise approach with this group as well.
---
|
---
| Please note that there is no action that older adults need to take at this time to get a vaccine. When we are ready to start vaccinating this population, we will communicate with the public, healthcare providers, and community organizations to provide instructions. We will also continue to share updates through this newsletter and other outlets.
While the vaccine rollout will take time, there is a lot you can do in the meantime to protect yourself and others from COVID-19. Testing is more available now than it has ever been in Rhode Island. If you go online right now to [portal.ri.gov](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-ft4xkKCSBx8WvrTjurTymyvPkppZxmyvxTl9PG9FhoTIHSsncm_qsk7fJnLq71wx5BQweTrIhS5zPtywHFDWVCJamGAfr_8tfw3D9PZ4rxxZuwVfUkJsolSvr0YAtyB4pHV9GcX7lw=&c=A1_MvXMmrIevMJQwWTCm-CDieowSt_XU8xkuhC7_oI72FLQ-kYhP5A==&ch=rEklWNiYKABmwLJfXmF8FQZRNmtJjqMvtmyFHH2CL93ZoqtWkoAx5w==), you can make a same-day appointment to get tested at many sites throughout the state. If you test positive, Rhode Island also has a new doctor-recommended treatment. This fast, easy, and highly effective treatment helps keep you from getting sicker and being hospitalized. However, the earlier you start treatment, the more effective it is. If you test positive for COVID-19 and are 65 or older or have an underlying health condition, immediately call your healthcare provider and ask about treatment for COVID-19. You can find out more information about this treatment [here](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-ft4xkKCSBx8WvrTjurTymyvPkppZxmyvxTl9PG9FhoTIHSsncm_qnGKv7eNuRYwxEsUik9fjW7CgHpQinwr6C2f2cE2V093emYgxKU2xZAcj11rFitcdYqnQtis5TiZi3677ryOJiZLKgQ6GnRBeZqtSJyNKkcy3C_9TmyzFgn2c-xkSx-LNg==&c=A1_MvXMmrIevMJQwWTCm-CDieowSt_XU8xkuhC7_oI72FLQ-kYhP5A==&ch=rEklWNiYKABmwLJfXmF8FQZRNmtJjqMvtmyFHH2CL93ZoqtWkoAx5w==).
Lastly, I want to echo the sentiments shared by Dr. Alexander-Scott at yesterday's COVID-19 press conference and thank everyone. It's been a very long 10 months. I think we've all learned a lot. Crisis is a very powerful teacher. One thing that we've seen and learned time and time again is that we do so much better when we come together and support each other. Our goal is to offer a COVID-19 vaccine to everyone in Rhode Island as quickly as we can. We all want to be healthy and safe, to keep our loved ones healthy and safe, and to get COVID-19 behind us. We are getting there. Thank you for your patience and sacrifices to this point. We need to keep it going for a little while longer, and we need to remember that we're all in this together. So please, let's keep supporting each other.
We will continue to share regular updates as more information becomes available. You can find updates on vaccination planning and answers to frequently asked questions on [RIDOH's COVID-19 Vaccine page](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-ft4xkKCSBx8WvrTjurTymyvPkppZxmyvxTl9PG9FhoTIHSsncm_qnGKv7eNuRYwrMJIOsLw6X3DPrNiyepucG8aKWyA1Do1dZ1hYmIkNbTuBASiA2CYlBBQFA8WF8sdLl6ycKy8QOI6sxnBiUM80DYfvak1A3G6&c=A1_MvXMmrIevMJQwWTCm-CDieowSt_XU8xkuhC7_oI72FLQ-kYhP5A==&ch=rEklWNiYKABmwLJfXmF8FQZRNmtJjqMvtmyFHH2CL93ZoqtWkoAx5w==).
Philip A. Chan, MD, MS Consultant Medical Director, RIDOH
---
|
---
| | Governor's Office | 82 Smith Street, Providence, RI 02903
---

---

You can find out more information about this treatment ==. Lastly, I want to echo the sentiments shared by Dr. Alexander-Scott at yesterday's COVID-19 press conference and thank everyone. It's been a very long 10 months. I think we've all learned a lot. Crisis is a very powerful teacher. One thing that we've seen and learned time and time again is that we do so much better when we come together and support each other. Our goal is to offer a COVID-19 vaccine to everyone in Rhode Island as quickly as we can. We all want to be healthy and safe, to keep our loved ones healthy and safe, and to get COVID-19 behind us. We are getting there. Thank you for your patience and sacrifices to this point. We need to keep it going for a little while longer, and we need to remember that we're all in this together. So please, let's keep supporting each other. We will continue to share regular updates as more information becomes available. You can find updates on vaccination planning and answers to frequently asked questions on ==. Philip A. Chan, MD, MS Consultant Medical Director, RIDOH | | | Governor's Office | 82 Smith Street, Providence, RI 02903 | | Sent by mailto:communications@governor.ri.gov powered by

subject: DNC Updates [January 22, 2021]
body:
| | | |
---
| | | Dear Friends- I am still taking in all that we have accomplished as Democrats. This week was the culmination of years of hard work across constituencies united in a common purpose. Seeing our Democratic elected officials and an inauguration that withstood the recent physical attacks held particular significance. As a country, we also had a moment to pause and reflect on the lives lost to COVID, something we had not done before as a collective. I thought about my own departed friend Dr. Sean Boynes, a fellow Howard University graduate and a pharmacist who felt compelled to keep serving his community through this pandemic. But his asthma made him especially vulnerable when he contracted COVID-19--leaving behind a wife and two little girls. I know his spirit was beaming at the swearing in of this Biden and (Howard Alumna) Harris administration. But even with our new leadership, Trump’s utter mismanagement could still lead to upwards of 100,000 more Sean’s taken from us while the Biden-Harris Administration works around the clock to bring this under control. And press reports yesterday confirmed what we already knew: The Trump administration had no vaccine plan and left no blueprint for the Biden administration. Only about 14 million Americans have been vaccinated — far below the 20 million Trump’s administration promised to have completed three weeks ago. However, President Biden and his team are setting us on a new course. They’re centralizing their operation and the President’s COVID-19 Vaccination Plan “American Rescue” is already underway. It includes an ambitious goal to administer 100 million shots by the end of his first 100 days. The leadership that was sorely lacking for the last 4 years, is now being demonstrated. President Biden’s plan will get $1,400 direct relief checks to Americans, on top of $600 checks previously approved for a total of $2,000 in direct aid; enhance unemployment benefits and extend pandemic unemployment programs; increase the minimum wage to $15 an hour; provide additional support for small businesses; extend the federal eviction moratorium and provide additional aid to low- and moderate-income households out of work; and provide funding for vaccines, testing, and schools. In addition, the new administration and our Democratic Senate majority got to work immediately addressing other critical issues left in the wake of Donald Trump. President Biden signed several executive orders, many of them undoing the damage inflicted upon our nation. This week the Senate began to confirm President Biden’s cabinet nominees. So far, the process has yielded the confirmation of Retired Gen. Lloyd Austin as the Secretary of Defense and Avril Haines as the Director of National Intelligence. Here at the Democratic National Committee, the work also continues. Yesterday the DNC members met virtually for the Winter Meeting. At this meeting, the DNC’s officers for the 2021-2025 term assumed their new positions. The DNC’s new slate of leadership:
* Jaime Harrison, DNC Chair
* Mayor Keisha Lance Bottoms, Vice Chair of Civic Engagement and Voter Protection
* Governor Gretchen Whitmer, Vice Chair
* Senator Tammy Duckworth, Vice Chair
* Representative Filemon Vela Jr., Vice Chair
* Ken Martin, Vice Chair for ASDC
* Jason Rae, Secretary
* Virginia McGregor, Treasurer
* Chris Korge, National Finance Chair

And we are very proud that the DNC announced its first senior staff hires: DNC Deputy CEO Sam Cornale will now serve as our Executive Director, and DNC CEO and veteran campaign leader Mary Beth Cahill will continue her service to the party as Senior Advisor. I look forward to what this new Biden-Harris administration and the new DNC leadership, led by Chair Jaime Harrison, is poised to accomplish. I know we can expect great things to come! Yours in the Fight, Mũthoni In this Update
* President Biden’s Executive Actions
* New DNC Leadership Elected
* DNC Announces First Senior Staff Hires

---
|
---
| **Executive Actions: ** President Biden has wasted no time in combating the challenges facing America. He has signed 17 executive actions. Some of the actions taken address the pandemic, immigration, climate change, LGBTQ+ equality, diversity and inclusion, as well as the economy. **On the Pandemic** President Biden signed an [executive order](https://collegedems.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b3a52587106e5f265f227782b&id=2742f33377&e=d6c5c7d7ec) requiring social distancing and the wearing of masks on all federal property and by all federal employees. He is also reinstating ties with the World Health Organization after the Trump administration chose to [withdraw the nation’s membership](https://collegedems.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b3a52587106e5f265f227782b&id=c60c60eab7&e=d6c5c7d7ec) and funding last year. **On Immigration and Visas** President Biden signed an [executive order](https://collegedems.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b3a52587106e5f265f227782b&id=62280b9c56&e=d6c5c7d7ec) preserving and fortifying the Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals program that protects from deportation immigrants brought to the United States as children, often called Dreamers. Mr. Trump sought for years to end the program, known as DACA. The order also calls on Congress to enact legislation providing permanent status and a path to citizenship for those immigrants. **On Climate Change** President Biden signed an executive order to [rejoin the Paris climate agreement](https://collegedems.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b3a52587106e5f265f227782b&id=0fda9f4a14&e=d6c5c7d7ec), which it will officially rejoin 30 days from now. In 2019, the previous administration [notified the United Nations that the United States would withdraw](https://collegedems.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b3a52587106e5f265f227782b&id=a2b0f8d827&e=d6c5c7d7ec) from the coalition of nearly 200 countries working to move away from planet-warming fossil fuels like coal, oil and natural gas. **On LGBTQ+ Equality** Yesterday, [President Biden signed an Executive Order](https://collegedems.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b3a52587106e5f265f227782b&id=7019092e3d&e=d6c5c7d7ec) implementing the Supreme Court’s historic decision last June in Bostock v. Clayton County that made it clear, once and for all, that discrimination on the basis of sexual orientation and gender identity is prohibited under federal sex nondiscrimination laws. **On Diversity and Inclusion ** President Biden [revoked](https://collegedems.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b3a52587106e5f265f227782b&id=6696170fb5&e=d6c5c7d7ec) Mr. Trump’s executive order limiting the ability of federal agencies, contractors and other institutions to hold diversity and inclusion training. **On the Economy** President Biden extended the [moratorium on evictions](https://collegedems.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b3a52587106e5f265f227782b&id=209676a4a5&e=d6c5c7d7ec) through the end of March on his first day to provide relief to renters struggling during the pandemic At the request of President Biden, the Acting Secretary of Education will extend the [pause on federal student loan payments](https://collegedems.us12.list-manage.com/track/click?u=b3a52587106e5f265f227782b&id=4c6d0f7828&e=d6c5c7d7ec) and collections and keep the interest rate at 0%.
---
|
---
| ** DNC Announces First Senior Staff Hires** Today the Democratic National Committee is announcing its first senior staff hires following President Biden’s inauguration and the selection of DNC Chair Jaime Harrison: Sam Cornale will serve as Executive Director, and Mary Beth Cahill will continue her service to the party as Senior Advisor. At the DNC, this leadership team will work to grow President Biden’s political operation and infrastructure, advance the agenda of the Biden-Harris administration, and lay the groundwork for a successful midterm election for Democrats at every level of the ballot. The DNC will announce additional senior staff in the weeks ahead -- continuing the Committee's commitment to building a senior leadership team which reflects the Democratic Party and incorporates its top political talent. “Under Mary Beth’s leadership the DNC played a critical role in electing President Biden and Vice President Harris, and their continued work with Sam at the helm will ensure President Biden’s commitment to a strong DNC and Democratic party infrastructure is fulfilled,” **said former Biden Campaign Manager Jen O’Malley Dillon.** “With incoming Chair Harrison, this team will continue to support the vital work of grassroots Democrats, advance the priorities voters endorsed by electing the Biden-Harris administration, and set the stage for Democrats’ continued success in the campaigns ahead.” “These battle-tested leaders know how to win, build a political operation that is second to none, and I’m excited they will lead the critical work of the DNC,” **said incoming DNC Chair Jaime Harrison.** “Their continued work will help fulfill the mission of the DNC: growing Democrats’ infrastructure in every part of every state, standing up for the principles we believe in, and marshalling the full resources of the Democratic Party in support of the Biden-Harris administration.” “In 2020 a revitalized DNC helped President Biden and Vice President Harris win a historic race for the White House -- and helped Democrats flip the Senate, hold the House and strengthen our party at every level. This work is important, not just to winning elections but to improving people’s lives,” **said DNC Executive Director Sam Cornale.** “President Biden and my friend incoming Chair Harrison are committed to investing in state parties and our grassroots, building a top-notch political infrastructure in every zip code, and making sure our organization reflects the diverse voices of our great Democratic family. I look forward to working with Chairman Harrison to build a team reflective of the diversity of our party, and one that will work tirelessly to make these goals a reality and lead Democrats to future success.” “The effective, hard work done at the DNC helped win the White House and secure Democratic victories up and down the ticket -- now we need to redouble our efforts,” **said DNC Senior Advisor Mary Beth Cahill.** “A well run and strategic DNC is the foundation for Democrats’ success everywhere, and I couldn’t be more excited to continue this important work in support of the Biden-Harris administration and the entire Democratic Party.” “These leaders will build on the successes of the DNC during the last four years, and take our party forward with the purpose, tactics and values that all Democrats will be proud of,” **said outgoing DNC Chair Tom Perez.** “Along with Jaime and our new DNC officers, they will bring their invaluable experience and dedication to the task of growing and leading a strong, vibrant Democratic Party under President Biden and Vice President Harris. This week during his inauguration, Americans saw again that President Biden is our Healer in Chief and represents the best of our party’s values -- a focus on making life better, more fair and more prosperous for more Americans. I know this team will continue to do everything in their power to fight alongside this administration for these principles.” Biographies are below: **Sam Cornale** Sam Cornale is one of the top political, communications and campaign strategists in the Democratic Party, with more than a decade of experience on campaigns and across the Federal government. Cornale currently serves as the Deputy CEO at the Democratic National Committee, overseeing the operations of the DNC's communications, opinion research, fundraising, and data and technology work. Prior to serving the DNC, Cornale managed DNC Chair Tom Perez’s successful campaign to lead the Democratic Party, an effort that raised a seven figure sum in just six weeks and resulted in a come-from-behind victory. Cornale is a veteran of the Obama Administration, having served multiple cabinet secretaries as a speechwriter and as the Deputy Chief of Staff to then-Labor Secretary Tom Perez. Cornale is also a veteran of the 2008 and 2012 Obama campaigns, with experience in Wisconsin and Pennsylvania, and the 2016 campaign of Secretary Hillary Clinton in North Carolina. Cornale earned his degree in political science from the University of Wisconsin-Madison. He is a native of Madison, Wisconsin, and an obsessive follower of the Wisconsin Badgers and Green Bay Packers. Cornale serves as a lecturer at the University of Wisconsin, teaching a political science course on the evolution of media and its impact on presidential campaigns and governance. He lives in Washington, D.C., with his two-year-old Ridgeback, June. **Mary Beth Cahill** Mary Beth Cahill has spent her professional life in public service, has established herself as a leader in Democratic politics, and is currently the DNC’s CEO. She served as Campaign Manager for John Kerry for President, Chief of Staff to Senator Edward Kennedy, and prior to that, as Assistant to the President and Director of Public Liaison in Bill Clinton's White House. Cahill successfully managed Edward J. Markey's campaign for a seat in the United States House of Representatives, and then went on to manage Senate campaigns for Patrick Leahy in Vermont, Claiborne Pell in Rhode Island and Les AuCoin in Oregon. In between campaign activity, Cahill served in various positions for the Governor of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts, including Director of Federal-State Relations under Governor Michael Dukakis and Director of the Governor's Personnel Office. Cahill is recognized as an expert on Women and Politics. For over five years, she served as Executive Director of EMILY's List, and has trained female candidates for political office across the globe. Cahill has received numerous awards and honors. She was named a "Mover and Shaker" by Campaigns and Elections magazine, and recognized as one of the POLITICS "Fabulous Fifty" by Roll Call. Cahill also was honored as one of the Top Political Action in Leaders in America by Campaigns and Elections. After graduating from Emmanuel College, she began her political career as a staff associate and receptionist for former Congressman Robert F. Drinan (D-Mass.). ###

I look forward to working with Chairman Harrison to build a team reflective of the diversity of our party, and one that will work tirelessly to make these goals a reality and lead Democrats to future success.” “The effective, hard work done at the DNC helped win the White House and secure Democratic victories up and down the ticket -- now we need to redouble our efforts,” said DNC Senior Advisor Mary Beth Cahill. “A well run and strategic DNC is the foundation for Democrats’ success everywhere, and I couldn’t be more excited to continue this important work in support of the Biden-Harris administration and the entire Democratic Party.” “These leaders will build on the successes of the DNC during the last four years, and take our party forward with the purpose, tactics and values that all Democrats will be proud of,” said outgoing DNC Chair Tom Perez. “Along with Jaime and our new DNC officers, they will bring their invaluable experience and dedication to the task of growing and leading a strong, vibrant Democratic Party under President Biden and Vice President Harris. This week during his inauguration, Americans saw again that President Biden is our Healer in Chief and represents the best of our party’s values -- a focus on making life better, more fair and more prosperous for more Americans. I know this team will continue to do everything in their power to fight alongside this administration for these principles.” Biographies are below: Sam Cornale Sam Cornale is one of the top political, communications and campaign strategists in the Democratic Party, with more than a decade of experience on campaigns and across the Federal government. Cornale currently serves as the Deputy CEO at the Democratic National Committee, overseeing the operations of the DNC's communications, opinion research, fundraising, and data and technology work. Prior to serving the DNC, Cornale managed DNC Chair Tom Perez’s successful campaign to lead the Democratic Party, an effort that raised a seven figure sum in just six weeks and resulted in a come-from-behind victory. Cornale is a veteran of the Obama Administration, having served multiple cabinet secretaries as a speechwriter and as the Deputy Chief of Staff to then-Labor Secretary Tom Perez. Cornale is also a veteran of the 2008 and 2012 Obama campaigns, with experience in Wisconsin and Pennsylvania, and the 2016 campaign of Secretary Hillary Clinton in North Carolina. Cornale earned his degree in political science from the University of Wisconsin-Madison. He is a native of Madison, Wisconsin, and an obsessive follower of the Wisconsin Badgers and Green Bay Packers. Cornale serves as a lecturer at the University of Wisconsin, teaching a political science course on the evolution of media and its impact on presidential campaigns and governance. He lives in Washington, D.C., with his two-year-old Ridgeback, June. Mary Beth Cahill Mary Beth Cahill has spent her professional life in public service, has established herself as a leader in Democratic politics, and is currently the DNC’s CEO. She served as Campaign Manager for John Kerry for President, Chief of Staff to Senator Edward Kennedy, and prior to that, as Assistant to the President and Director of Public Liaison in Bill Clinton's White House. Cahill successfully managed Edward J. Markey's campaign for a seat in the United States House of Representatives, and then went on to manage Senate campaigns for Patrick Leahy in Vermont, Claiborne Pell in Rhode Island and Les AuCoin in Oregon. In between campaign activity, Cahill served in various positions for the Governor of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts, including Director of Federal-State Relations under Governor Michael Dukakis and Director of the Governor's Personnel Office. Cahill is recognized as an expert on Women and Politics. For over five years, she served as Executive Director of EMILY's List, and has trained female candidates for political office across the globe. Cahill has received numerous awards and honors. She was named a "Mover and Shaker" by Campaigns and Elections magazine, and recognized as one of the POLITICS "Fabulous Fifty" by Roll Call. Cahill also was honored as one of the Top Political Action in Leaders in America by Campaigns and Elections. After graduating from Emmanuel College, she began her political career as a staff associate and receptionist for former Congressman Robert F. Drinan (D-Mass.). ### | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | This email was sent to mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com DNC Community Engagement 430 S Capitol Street, SE Washington, DC 20003 * USA

clean_body: | 46 Peter — Can you believe it? President Joe Biden and Vice President Kamala Harris?! Cue the confetti!! | Their inauguration marks a historic moment for our country, and each and every one of you played a role in making this happen! Now, it’s time to get back to work, and refocus on what’s next. So, whether you are running for office, serving as a local leader, or working on a campaign, check out this week’s TL;DR and get yourself plugged in to our upcoming events and latest news. — The NDTC Team Virtual Live Trainings: Week of January 25 Monday, January 25 at 1pm ET Gain an understanding of organizational structure for your local Democratic organization, and walk you through examples to determine what is best for your group's goals in 2021. Tuesday, January 26 at 8pm ET This could be a big year for your local Democratic organization. Join us for this free training to learn how you can grow your group in 2021. We’ll guide you through conversations with potential recruits, and how to make a plan to recruit people who will help you meet your 2021 goals. Thursday, January 28 at 1pm ET If we want the Democratic party to win in your community, we need to continue organizing regardless of whether there’s an election around the corner or not. Join us for this training to learn more about the work you can do to organize in 2021.
